world.title: Ukraine war briefing: First official ceasefire agreed after Zelenskyy push succeeds
world.description: Vladimir Putin paints Easter pause in fighting as Russia’s idea, but Ukraine’s president made repeated offers. What we know on day 1,507

world.title: The UAE alliance Australia won’t question
world.description: Australia’s deep military and political ties with the UAE expose it to risk while aligning with an autocratic partner.

world.title: 2030 Universal Education Target Out of Reach, UNESCO Warns
world.description: UNESCO's latest report warns the 2030 universal education target is unattainable, with 272 million children still out of school despite past progress.

world.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran war ceasefire does not cover Lebanon, Netanyahu says; Hezbollah launches rockets at Israel
world.description: Israeli prime minister’s remarks come shortly after Trump told US media he had asked Netanyahu to be more ‘low-key’ amid shaky Iran ceasefire

world.title: 'I'm not Epstein's victim' and 'We see you, Vlad'
world.description: US First Lady Melania Trump is splashed across several of the papers, after she made a surprise White House address on Thursday.

world.title: Tanzanian leader orders smaller convoys and shared buses to cut fuel use as prices rise
world.description: Several African governments have announced steps to cope with fuel shortages and rising prices, one being Madagascar, which declared a state of emergency.

world.title: Strait of Hormuz still seeing significantly lower traffic despite Iran war ceasefire
world.description: Few ships passed through the waterway Wednesday and Thursday despite a ceasefire agreement that was supposed to open the waterway key to transiting oil across the globe.

world.title: How Russia's Network Collapse Exposes the Limits of Nuclear Deterrence
world.description: Russia's recourse to the Security Council veto appears to reflect not a choice made from strength but a structural dependency.

business.title: IFS and NEC to Deploy Next-Generation Core System IFS Cloud for Hoshizaki
business.description: IFS AB (IFS), the leading provider of Industrial AI software, and NEC Corporation (NEC; TSE: 6701) have announced that Hoshizaki Corporation, a world-leading manufacturer of commercial foodservice equipment, will implement IFS Cloud as its next-generation core system to transform its legacy ERP systems.

business.title: Ringgit extends gains on improved risk appetite
business.description: With the US economy on a weaker trajectory, as shown by GDP and consumption data, this could help the ringgit, says an analyst.

business.title: Synergy says ‘no evidence’ 900,000 customer accounts have been hacked despite warning
business.description: Earlier this week, cybersecurity websites and social media accounts reported a hacker under the moniker “hackboy” had stolen data from 900,002 accounts.

business.title: GIFT Nifty up 70 points; here's the trading setup for today's session
business.description: Indian stock markets saw a dip on Thursday. Investors are watching developments in West Asia. Near-term support might come from easing tensions and stable oil prices. However, a sustained recovery depends on the US-Iran situation, energy prices, and liquidity. The India VIX, a fear gauge, increased. Foreign investors were net sellers.

business.title: Stocks in news: RIL, TCS, IDFC First Bank, BPCL, SpiceJet
business.description: Indian equity markets saw profit booking on April 9, ending a five-day rally. Several companies like RIL, TCS, and IDFC First Bank are in focus due to news and Q4 results. Granules India faces US regulator scrutiny, while SpiceJet is ordered to pay $8 million to an engine lessor.

business.title: Anthropic Model Scare Sparks Urgent Bessent, Powell Warning to Bank CEOs
business.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to an urgent meeting on concerns that the latest artificial intelligence model from Anthropic PBC will usher in an era of greater cyber risk.

business.title: Oil Trims Gain, Asia Stocks Rise Before Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
business.description: (Bloomberg) -- Oil pared gains and most Asian stocks rose as investors positioned ahead of US-Iran ceasefire talks scheduled to take place in Pakistan this weekend.

business.title: Trains delayed on Perth’s Yanchep Line after railcar hits issues at Glendalough station
business.description: Commuters have hit delays on the Yanchep Line this morning.

science.title: FirstUp: Artemis II crew to return to Earth; EU's new travel rules to kick in... The news today
science.description: The four Artemis II astronauts are set to return to Earth today (April 10). The crew will splash down off the coast of San Diego, California. Also, the EU’s new Entry/Exit System (EES) will become fully operational today. The system will replace passport stamping with digitally recorded entries and exits

science.title: Artemis II could unlock a new understanding of the 5 hazards of space travel
science.description: Experiments aboard Orion are capturing insights about astronaut health that were never recorded during the Apollo program.

science.title: Scientists Spot a Solar Flare With Surprising Spectral Behavior
science.description: On August 19, 2022, astronomers using the Daniel K. Inouye Solar Telescope (DKIST) on the Hawaiian island of Maui caught the fading remnants of a C-class solar flare. Their observations showed something unusual: very strong spectral fingerprints of calcium II H and hydrogen-epsilon lines. It was the first time these two light signatures were seen in great detail during a flare. According to computer models, those lines were stronger than expected and play a not well-understood role in how flares heat the solar atmosphere where they occur.

science.title: The riskiest moments of NASA's Artemis II mission may still be ahead
science.description: The astronauts on NASA's Artemis II moon mission are scheduled to land on Earth on Friday. But their re-entry is one of the riskiest parts of the mission, and the Orion spacecraft has known design flaws.

science.title: Earth's 'oldest octopus' was not actually an octopus after all
science.description: Scientists uncover the truth behind a well-known fossil, Pohlsepia mazonensis, showing the "world's oldest octopus" was not an octopus.

science.title: Artemis moon missions a ‘gift that keeps on giving’ for research
science.description: Scientists celebrate impact of pioneering space flight, with data and samples gathered predicted to shape studies for decades to come

science.title: Artemis II in photos: around the moon and inside Orion
science.description: The dozens of photos taken by the astronauts include never before seen views of the moon, a total solar eclipse and a stunning shot of Earth.

science.title: Artemis II crew to return home as Nasa lays out steps for safe splashdown
science.description: Astronauts prepare for re-entry several miles off coast of southern California after 10-day lunar fly-by mission

ai.title: Here’s where HR and finance aren’t in lockstep, according to Deloitte data
ai.description: Two major AI studies reveal a strategic disconnect between HR and finance that many organisations have not noticed yet.

ai.title: TCS Q4 profit up 12%, revenue rises 9.6% amidst AI growth and deal wins
ai.description: Tata Consultancy Services reported a 12% rise in net profit to Rs 13,718 crore for the March quarter, exceeding analyst expectations. Despite a 2.4% annual revenue decline in constant currency for FY26, the company saw strong AI services revenue growth and secured significant deal wins, signaling a potential turnaround.

ai.title: TCS’s financial performance under AI stress: Ready to shape up rather than ship out?
ai.description: TCS’s future may not be as dismal as dismissals of its business model suggest. Its revenue and profit growth raise optimism over the Indian software industry’s ability to survive and thrive in the era of artificial intelligence.

ai.title: TCS and five other firms empanelled to build & run AI for govt departments
ai.description: The Centre has empanelled six partner firms, including Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), to develop and deploy AI solutions across government departments. More than 80 companies had submitted bids for the request for empanelment (RFE), which closed last week, with firms such as KPMG, Deloitte, PwC, EY, Fractal Analytics, Gnani AI and Jio Haptik missing the final shortlist on February 27.

ai.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
ai.description: The ChatGPT-maker testified in favor of an Illinois bill that would limit when AI labs can be held liable—even in cases where their products cause “critical harm.”

ai.title: Pro-Iran groups have used AI to troll Trump and try to control the war narrative
ai.description: Analysts say memes seem to be coming from groups linked to the government in Tehran and are part of a strategy of leveraging its limited resources to inflict damage on the U.S.
